description_long: This object consists of three mushrooms with large, rounded caps that are bright red and covered in irregular white spots. The largest mushroom dominates the group, with a wide, slightly domed cap and a thick, pale stem. Two smaller mushrooms cluster at its base, each with similarly colored caps and shorter, stouter stems. The caps have a smooth, matte finish with a distinct spotted pattern, reminiscent of the Amanita muscaria species. The stems are cylindrical and taper slightly toward the base, with a muted beige color. No gills are visible beneath the caps, and the mushrooms appear to be resting on an undefined, flat surface.
description: A cluster of three red mushrooms with white spots, featuring thick pale stems and smooth, domed caps. The largest mushroom is central, flanked by two smaller ones at its base.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three mushrooms, with the largest cap prominently displayed and both smaller mushrooms clearly visible at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the largest mushroom and one smaller mushroom, with the third mushroom partially obscured.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the largest mushroom cap and its thick stem, with the smaller mushrooms mostly hidden.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the largest mushroom and both smaller mushrooms, with the stems and cap edges visible.
